contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped human approval, task assignment, escalation, decision capture, audit, metrics, and delegation controls
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-27 keeps human-interaction-gate as the portability/UI/package slug while current runtime services live under server/src/engine/flows/human-approval-gate. Fork assembly copies that implementation directory into the canonical human-interaction-gate package path.
coreGuarantees: Approval requests are stored before reviewer notification events.
Decision capture remains idempotent and rejects duplicate decisions.
Timeout handling escalates overdue work without blocking the live path.
Human task assignment records deadline, priority, and assignee history.
SLA metrics and analytics remain asynchronous.
Scheduled triggers are deduplicated by schedule identity.
Sequential and parallel approval chains preserve configured ordering.
Approval gates stop workflow progress until a valid human decision exists.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-27 controls human approval gates and workflow stoppage decisions, so Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

machineLockedRules: approval request is stored before notification
timeout escalation never deletes pending approval history
approval gate cannot be bypassed by tenant configuration
scheduled and delegated tasks remain idempotent

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-27 operating values. Tenants may tune reviewer labels, notification topics, timeout thresholds, chain labels, delegation depth, schedule horizon, and module naming without changing tenant isolation, approval-gate enforcement, idempotency, audit ordering, or package provenance requirements.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to review queue naming, timeout and delegation thresholds, notification topics, escalation group labels, schedule horizons, SLA display thresholds, and module naming.

edgeCases.name: Acme approval operations
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes the human gate for strict compliance approvals with short SLA thresholds and shallow delegation.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: timeouts, SLA threshold, delegation depth, module naming
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Approval-gate enforcement, idempotency, tenant isolation, and audit ordering remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind review router
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes the package for broader parallel review and operations escalation queues.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: parallel threshold, escalation group, review queue label
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Sequential/parallel chain semantics and gate enforcement remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Tessera human gate studio
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes schedule horizons and notification topics for collaborative review studios.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: schedule horizon, notification topic, module naming
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Append-only audit, tenant scope, and package provenance remain fixed.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T413 through T422 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1103 through F1122 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Tenant scope is mandatory for every approval, task, chain, schedule, delegation, audit, and metric record.
Approval requests are stored before notification events.
Approval gates cannot be bypassed by tenant configuration.
Package provenance and GitHub Actions CI evidence remain required for certification.

tenantAApprovalOps.description: Tenant A tunes Human Interaction Gate for strict compliance approval operations.
tenantAApprovalOps.impact: Tenant A gets stricter approval defaults while gate enforcement, tenant isolation, idempotency, audit ordering, and package provenance stay unchanged.
tenantAApprovalOps.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-27 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-27 services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses after fork assembly.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
